sections.paragraph: (“whose people are many”), Yarobeam (for so it should be pronounced, not Yorobeam), [Jeroboam], pr.n. of two kings of the ten tribes, the former of whom, the son of Nebat, was the founder of the kingdom of Ephraim and of Moscholatry; he reigned 975-54, B. C., -1 Ki. 1214 ; the other was the son of Jehoaz, 825-784. -2 Ki. 14:2329 .
